Making sourdough bread at home is one of the most rewarding baking experiences. In this guide, we’ll walk you through every step.

Getting Started with Your Starter

A sourdough starter is a living culture of wild yeast and beneficial bacteria. It takes about 7 days to create one from scratch.

The Basic Recipe

  • 500g bread flour
  • 350g water
  • 100g active starter
  • 10g salt

Mix the flour and water, let it rest for 30 minutes (autolyse), then add the starter and salt. For more details, visit our baking classes.

Pro Tips

Use a Dutch oven for the best crust. Preheat it to 250°C (482°F) for at least 30 minutes.

[gallery ids="101,102,103"]

The secret to great bread is time and patience.

Temperature: 250°C
Time: 25 min covered + 20 min uncovered

Happy baking! — Maria
